text:
🇦🇺 AUSTRALIA — TRANSACTION COSTS 
1. Why this matters
Defines total acquisition cost, distinguishes mandatory government charges from negotiable costs, and identifies additional costs applicable to foreign buyers.
2. Official Government Fees (Mandatory)
(Official Fact)
Transfer duties (stamp duty) are imposed at the state and territory level, with rates varying by jurisdiction.
Rates vary by state
Generally calculated as a percentage of property value
There is no unified national transfer tax; all acquisition duties are determined at the state and territory level.
(Official Fact)
Foreign buyers are subject to additional surcharge purchaser duty:
Example:
New South Wales → ~8% surcharge
Rates vary across states
(Official Fact)
Foreign persons must pay an application fee for FIRB approval:
Fee amount depends on property value
Payable prior to acquisition approval
(Advisory Note)
These charges are mandatory for foreign buyers and significantly increase total transaction cost.
3. Common Market Practice Costs (Negotiable)
(Market Practice)
Real Estate Agent / Broker Fees:
Typically 1.5% – 3% of property value
Usually paid by seller (not always by buyer)
(Advisory Note)
Fees vary by state and transaction structure
Negotiable between parties
4. Optional Professional Costs
(Market Practice / Optional)
Legal / Conveyancing Fees:
Typically AUD 1,000 – AUD 3,000+ depending on complexity
(Advisory Note)
Legal representation is not legally mandatory, but commonly used
Conveyancing is standard practice in most transactions
5. Advisory Caution
(Advisory Caution)
Transaction costs vary significantly by:
State or territory
Property value
Foreign buyer status
(Advisory Caution)
Foreign buyers face materially higher acquisition costs due to:
Surcharge duties
FIRB application fees
6. What Needs Confirmation (Transaction-Level)
Applicable state-level stamp duty rates
Foreign buyer surcharge applicable in specific state
FIRB application fee based on property value
Legal and conveyancing fees for specific transaction
7. Sources
https://foreigninvestment.gov.au
8. Verification Date
04/04/2026
9. Escalation Note
Not Required –
Transaction cost structure is clearly defined. Variations arise from state-level differences and property value, not from unclear or conflicting rules.
